channel.title: Comments for page "Not My Circus Not My Monkeys"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Not My Circus Not My Monkeys"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"ah yes. rainbow theme is for pride month yes? dado sees other businesses putting up rainbow logos to show they are good allies and make money. hamster think this goes 2 far. political corrective surgery run amuck. hamster say make america grate cheese again but dado think this not go far enough. dado make everything rainbow theme 2 show that dado is best ally. or best lgtv barbecue person. dado not care so long as dado make money. dado is all things 2 all people yes."

Look how they massacred my boy.

Ah! So this the the Pride Month dado you promised.

Thank you! +100

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have confirmation that iPhone users can view the text correctly on Desktop Mode. I request iPhone users wishing to read this do so using Desktop Mode, at least for the time being. If Lordstonefish wants to play around with the spans and find something that works across all platforms, he may, but my coding skills are limited.

I apologize for the inconvenience.

Edit: Thanks to stormbreath, the rainbow text should now work on iPhones, albeit slightly differently. Again, if you're still having trouble viewing this on an iPhone, please view it on desktop mode. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I realize the rainbow text isn't the easiest to read, but that's kind of the point. Both the reader and the characters are meant to find dado annoying.

this just reads like a pile of goofy crosslinks and references without any real purpose behind it other than being wacky.

yeppers.

Dread & Circuses also isn't set in 'the' SCP Universe, whatever that is. They travel all the Worlds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My two favorite gois, smashed together in a rainbow explosion of glorious lunacy. +1 you big, beautiful bastard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much agreeing on all counts here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yup, just confirmed this is indeed happening.

The author(s) may fix it by removing the following syntax

 [[span style="background: linear-gradient(to right, #FF7F7F, #FFB27F, #e6e600, #A5FF7F, #7FC9FF, #D67FFF, #FF7FED); -webkit-background-clip: text; -webkit-text-fill-color: transparent;"]]

and its related closed spans. It should work if each line is individually spanned instead of using the webkit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the links in the rainbow text are 100% impossible to see without mousing over them. also, this just reads like a pile of goofy crosslinks and references without any real purpose behind it other than being wacky. also also, the universe where the Rookie is from in Speed Demon doesn't have a veil, so referencing it in a tale set in the SCP universe doesn't really make much sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im on android and it all worked. This made me laugh a few times and terrified me too. Best mix.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tried running this on a simulated iOS just to see if I could fix it, but more details are necessary.
- model
- iOS version
- browser (i assume safari)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only the first line of colored text is visible on mobile for me (on an iPhone, if that gives any hint to a solution). It makes reading troublesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This actually ended about as well as it possibly could have. There was at least one point where I laughed out loud. dado is confirmed as an Eldritch Abomination ™, Manny is legitimately terrified, Moon Champion does his (?) Moon Champion thing, and Robert Mitchum eats lettuce. Definitely a +1, would trust dado again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this and SCP-4292 are my and LordStoneFish's entries for Collab-con2019! LordStoneFish is absent at the moment for unknown reasons, but A Random Day ruled that his contribution was still sufficient to count.

I'm also aware that our entries play the 'collaboration' element a little fast and loose, and if that disqualifies them so be it. I just couldn't imagine any collaboration between the Circus and dado ending well.

Thanks to everyone who looked these over.
